message:
feat(agent-builder): support authorized Agent Pack forks (#2845)

## Linear


https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-1215/agent-builder-%E6%94%AF%E6%8C%81-official-agent-%E5%B7%B2%E8%B4%AD%E6%A8%A1%E6%9D%BF-fork-%E4%BA%8C%E5%BC%80

## Summary

- Add requester-authorized Agent Pack Fork discovery for public Official
Packs, direct purchases, entitled hidden industry Packs, and active
Packs in the current organization.
- Create independent Agent Builder projects with immutable source
provenance, confirmed target identity, atomic target ID reservation, and
first-submission binding to a new Pack.
- Gate Fork on a compatible Agent Studio release/workspace and import
the approved source Submission directly from its trusted R2 asset.
- Make Submit recoverable when a Submission is created before its Pack
Test Run association is persisted.
- Replace Open Project/Open Builder entry points with one shared Fork
chooser and confirmation flow, while preserving the default Pack list
API contract.
- Add the implementation design and completed architecture review
findings.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-py.sh`
- [x] Backend Fork/repository/route tests: 173 passed
- [x] Full frontend unit suite: 568 files, 7,609 passed
- [x] Post-merge conflict regression: 83 passed
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Local frontend route responds on
`http://localhost:3000/agent-builder`
- [x] Mock capability, catalog Fork sources, and current-org sources
smoke-tested

## Rollout notes

- Removing `/agent-builder/projects/open` and the share URL/text import
flow is an intentional breaking cutover. Backend, frontend, and the
Fork-capable Agent Studio release must be coordinated; arbitrary
independent rollout order is not supported for this migration.
- The Fork chooser is controlled by the backend capability endpoint and
remains disabled for a stale or unavailable Agent Studio release.
- The corresponding Fork-capable Agent Studio beta must be available
before end-to-end staging validation.

## Size rationale

The reviewed diff is 3,319 lines after CI exclusions, 319 lines above
the standard limit. This is one cross-layer capability: server-side
entitlement enforcement and source resolution, immutable project/target
identity, Agent Studio compatibility gating, submit recovery, two
frontend entry points, mock support, and regression tests must land
together to avoid an enabled UI with an incomplete authorization or
publication path. The design document is already excluded from the CI
count, and the remaining size is primarily implementation plus coverage
for the security and recovery boundaries.
pr_body:
## Linear

https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-1215/agent-builder-%E6%94%AF%E6%8C%81-official-agent-%E5%B7%B2%E8%B4%AD%E6%A8%A1%E6%9D%BF-fork-%E4%BA%8C%E5%BC%80

## Summary

- Add requester-authorized Agent Pack Fork discovery for public Official Packs, direct purchases, entitled hidden industry Packs, and active Packs in the current organization.
- Create independent Agent Builder projects with immutable source provenance, confirmed target identity, atomic target ID reservation, and first-submission binding to a new Pack.
- Gate Fork on a compatible Agent Studio release/workspace and import the approved source Submission directly from its trusted R2 asset.
- Make Submit recoverable when a Submission is created before its Pack Test Run association is persisted.
- Replace Open Project/Open Builder entry points with one shared Fork chooser and confirmation flow, while preserving the default Pack list API contract.
- Add the implementation design and completed architecture review findings.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-py.sh`
- [x] Backend Fork/repository/route tests: 173 passed
- [x] Full frontend unit suite: 568 files, 7,609 passed
- [x] Post-merge conflict regression: 83 passed
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Local frontend route responds on `http://localhost:3000/agent-builder`
- [x] Mock capability, catalog Fork sources, and current-org sources smoke-tested

## Rollout notes

- Removing `/agent-builder/projects/open` and the share URL/text import flow is an intentional breaking cutover. Backend, frontend, and the Fork-capable Agent Studio release must be coordinated; arbitrary independent rollout order is not supported for this migration.
- The Fork chooser is controlled by the backend capability endpoint and remains disabled for a stale or unavailable Agent Studio release.
- The corresponding Fork-capable Agent Studio beta must be available before end-to-end staging validation.

## Size rationale

The reviewed diff is 3,319 lines after CI exclusions, 319 lines above the standard limit. This is one cross-layer capability: server-side entitlement enforcement and source resolution, immutable project/target identity, Agent Studio compatibility gating, submit recovery, two frontend entry points, mock support, and regression tests must land together to avoid an enabled UI with an incomplete authorization or publication path. The design document is already excluded from the CI count, and the remaining size is primarily implementation plus coverage for the security and recovery boundaries.
pr_title: feat(agent-builder): support authorized Agent Pack forks

message:
refactor(web): remove unused API routes (#2852)

## Summary
- remove 36 unreferenced legacy Next.js API route handlers together with
their route-only tests and stale auth/session declarations
- remove the unused `/api/connectors/*` stack, its unmounted Google
connector UI, helper, query key, dedicated icon, and tests
- keep the active `/api/composio-connectors/*` flow and the backend
OAuth worker callback unchanged
- update the Stripe setup guide so it no longer points at the removed
checkout-session lookup route

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh eslint.config.mjs
src/components/ui/icons/index.ts
src/components/ui/icons/providers/GitHub.tsx
tests/unit/hooks/queries/keys.unit.spec.ts
tests/unit/hooks/useIntegrations.unit.spec.ts`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`

## Size rationale
- request `size-override`: the size gate counts 4,024 changed lines, but
4,010 are deletions from removing unreferenced routes and their
route-only tests/components; the PR adds only 14 lines and keeps the
cleanup as one reviewable API-surface change
pr_body:
## Summary
- remove 36 unreferenced legacy Next.js API route handlers together with their route-only tests and stale auth/session declarations
- remove the unused `/api/connectors/*` stack, its unmounted Google connector UI, helper, query key, dedicated icon, and tests
- keep the active `/api/composio-connectors/*` flow and the backend OAuth worker callback unchanged
- update the Stripe setup guide so it no longer points at the removed checkout-session lookup route

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh eslint.config.mjs src/components/ui/icons/index.ts src/components/ui/icons/providers/GitHub.tsx tests/unit/hooks/queries/keys.unit.spec.ts tests/unit/hooks/useIntegrations.unit.spec.ts`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`

## Size rationale
- request `size-override`: the size gate counts 4,024 changed lines, but 4,010 are deletions from removing unreferenced routes and their route-only tests/components; the PR adds only 14 lines and keeps the cleanup as one reviewable API-surface change

message:
fix(sidenav): 优化侧边栏交互与视觉细节 (#2851)

## 说明

按照 ZooClaw Figma 设计稿优化侧边栏交互与视觉，不新增独立产品功能，也不关联 Linear。

## 主要修改

- 对齐主导航的字号、间距、Hover/选中背景、圆角、Logo 与头像尺寸，并补充 Library 入口及 User Guide 位置调整
- Agent 行仅保留 Hover 状态，展开后不保留背景；调整箭头、新建任务按钮及 Rename 交互
- 每个 Agent 默认展示 5 条记录（Session History 计入 5 条），Show More 每次增加 5 条，Show
Less 恢复为 5 条
- 恢复 session 的 Pin、More、Rename 与 Dropdown 交互；菜单打开期间保持灰色背景和操作按钮
- 对齐 session 的 31px 固定行高、6px 上下间距、18px 图标、颜色及文字选区；Rename 期间不再引起列表位移
- 修复收起侧边栏后深色模式 Logo 颜色，并补充 mock 场景与单元测试覆盖

## 验证

- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- 侧边栏相关单元测试：147 tests passed
- TypeScript 与 ESLint 通过
- localhost 人工验证 Hover、展开、分页、More Dropdown、Rename 和明暗主题状态

## 备注

- Pin 与 Delete 当前保留设计和交互入口，后端写操作由后续研发接入

---------

Co-authored-by: Lynn Zhuang <lynnzhuang@MacBook-Pro-2.local>

message:
feat(kb-sharing): lifecycle event source, reconcile cron, upload library selector (#2839)

## Linear
<!-- 无对应 issue；跨仓库设计规格随本 PR 落库 -->

## Summary

跨组织知识库共享（kb-sharing）的**事件源 + 前端**侧。设计规格与验收清单随本 PR
落库：`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-kb-sharing-design.md`（授权域在
`ecap-proxy-service#146`，需先合并部署）。

claw-interface 对 KB 保持零感知——只在生命周期节点向 proxy 报告事件，授权判定/存储/执行全在 proxy：

- `kb_grant_sync_service`：安装/卸载事件 best-effort（3s
超时、永不失败安装、`KB_GRANT_ADMIN_KEY` 未配置 = 特性整体关闭）；发布绑定注册 mandatory（拒绝即发布失败）
- 安装/卸载收尾接线：卸载前按 (uid, pack) 检查其他计算机上的活跃安装，避免误撤（评审 F3）
- 发布链：`kb_ref` 搭 `listing_metadata` 便车，Pack 生效前注册绑定；拒绝 →
`pack.kb_binding_refused`
- `/admin/cron/kb-grant-reconcile`：按 (uid, pack) 分组、全量分页扫描（无截断盲区，评审
F4），丢失事件/死机器安装收敛；`docs/cron-triggers.md` 同步更新
- BFF：`GET/POST /knowledge-base/kbs` 转发；`POST
/knowledge-base/kbs/{id}/editors` —— owner
按邮箱/手机号**精确匹配**添加编辑者（防枚举统一报错），解析 uid 后转发 proxy 显式授权端点（评审 F1 后的 v2.0
交接模型）
- web 上传页：库选择下拉 + 内联新建（乐观合并防 refetch 竞态），默认"不入库"= 现状行为逐字节不变
- Pack 模型、安装/分享主流程**零改动**

评审修复已含（对抗性评审 2026-07-13）：元数据触发的 editor 授权链删除（不可验证出处 =
自助提权）、多机聚合、对账全量分页、事件短超时、tsc 修复等——详见 spec 第 2/7 节。

## Test plan
- [x] 后端 unit：5486 passed（5 个失败为与本改动无关的环境基线：`test_aiohttp_leak_debug`
×4、deptry 包名映射 ×1，已用基线对照验证）
- [x] `lint-imports` 8/8 contracts KEPT、ruff、collection-string guard
- [x] web：知识库相关 34 passed + `tsc --noEmit` + eslint 全绿（verify-web）
- [x] sync 谓词全生命周期组合、对账分组/分页/瞬态跳过、防枚举 404、multipart kb_id 透传均有专项用例
- [ ] staging E2E：按 spec 第 6 节 14 项清单走查（依赖 GCP 前置：Vertex schema +
`KB_GRANT_ADMIN_KEY`）

## Rollout

合并顺序：`ecap-proxy-service#146` 先（无授权数据即零行为变化）→ 本 PR →
`ecap-agent-pack`（文档翻转，须在 key 配置后最后发）。紧急停用 = 从 secret 删除
`KB_GRANT_ADMIN_KEY`，无需回滚代码。

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
pr_body:

<!-- 无对应 issue；跨仓库设计规格随本 PR 落库 -->

## Summary

跨组织知识库共享（kb-sharing）的**事件源 + 前端**侧。设计规格与验收清单随本 PR 落库：`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-kb-sharing-design.md`（授权域在 `ecap-proxy-service#146`，需先合并部署）。

## Test plan
- [x] 后端 unit：5486 passed（5 个失败为与本改动无关的环境基线：`test_aiohttp_leak_debug` ×4、deptry 包名映射 ×1，已用基线对照验证）
- [x] `lint-imports` 8/8 contracts KEPT、ruff、collection-string guard
- [x] web：知识库相关 34 passed + `tsc --noEmit` + eslint 全绿（verify-web）
- [x] sync 谓词全生命周期组合、对账分组/分页/瞬态跳过、防枚举 404、multipart kb_id 透传均有专项用例
- [ ] staging E2E：按 spec 第 6 节 14 项清单走查（依赖 GCP 前置：Vertex schema + `KB_GRANT_ADMIN_KEY`）

## Rollout

合并顺序：`ecap-proxy-service#146` 先（无授权数据即零行为变化）→ 本 PR → `ecap-agent-pack`（文档翻转，须在 key 配置后最后发）。紧急停用 = 从 secret 删除 `KB_GRANT_ADMIN_KEY`，无需回滚代码。

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

message:
refactor(web): remove unused frontend modules (#2847)

## Summary
- remove the obsolete Cloudflare Pages Sentry middleware and its
`@sentry/cloudflare` dependency now that the app deploys through the
OpenNext worker
- remove production-unreachable frontend utilities, JSBridge modules,
and their test-only coverage
- remove stale storage/type/config references and ignore local
`.serena/` state

## Observability rationale
- The deployed app enters through `.open-next/worker.js` from
`wrangler.jsonc`; production and staging builds use
`opennextjs-cloudflare`.
- The deleted `functions/_middleware.ts` was a Cloudflare Pages
Functions entry. It was not imported or bundled by the OpenNext worker
path, so it was not providing server-side Sentry reporting before this
PR.
- Client-side Sentry remains unchanged, and Cloudflare Worker
observability remains enabled. Server-side Sentry for the OpenNext
worker is an existing gap, now documented explicitly in
`next.config.ts`, rather than a reporting path removed by this cleanup.
- Operational rollout is the normal web deployment: no worker entry,
route, or runtime handler changes. A future OpenNext-compatible server
Sentry integration should be implemented and validated as a separate
observability change.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/app run lint:deadcode`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/app exec vitest run
tests/unit/lib/auth/types.unit.spec.ts --config ./vitest.config.mts` (10
tests passed)
pr_body:
## Summary
- remove the obsolete Cloudflare Pages Sentry middleware and its `@sentry/cloudflare` dependency now that the app deploys through the OpenNext worker
- remove production-unreachable frontend utilities, JSBridge modules, and their test-only coverage
- remove stale storage/type/config references and ignore local `.serena/` state

## Observability rationale
- The deployed app enters through `.open-next/worker.js` from `wrangler.jsonc`; production and staging builds use `opennextjs-cloudflare`.
- The deleted `functions/_middleware.ts` was a Cloudflare Pages Functions entry. It was not imported or bundled by the OpenNext worker path, so it was not providing server-side Sentry reporting before this PR.
- Client-side Sentry remains unchanged, and Cloudflare Worker observability remains enabled. Server-side Sentry for the OpenNext worker is an existing gap, now documented explicitly in `next.config.ts`, rather than a reporting path removed by this cleanup.
- Operational rollout is the normal web deployment: no worker entry, route, or runtime handler changes. A future OpenNext-compatible server Sentry integration should be implemented and validated as a separate observability change.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/app run lint:deadcode`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/app exec vitest run tests/unit/lib/auth/types.unit.spec.ts --config ./vitest.config.mts` (10 tests passed)

message:
ci(arch-review): restore write tools via --allowedTools after action upgrade (#2846)

## Summary

修复 weekly Architectural Code Review
连续两周（[2026-07-06](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/28785274130)、[2026-07-13](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/29241552938)）全
matrix 失败的问题。

**根因**：#2737 把 `claude-code-action` 从 v1.0.88 升到 v1.0.165
时，`claude-arch-review.yaml` 的 `claude_args` 只传了 `--model`，没有像
`docs-maintenance.yml` 一样补 `--allowedTools`。v1.0.165 的 agent
模式默认不再放行写工具，模型对 `findings.md` 的 Write 全部被 permission-denied（失败 run 的 SDK
结果里 `permission_denials_count: 9`），三个模块都挂在 "Normalize finding values" 的
`findings.md not produced by review step`。

**修复**：给 `claude_args` 补上与 `docs-maintenance.yml` 一致的 `--allowedTools
"Bash,Edit,Write,Read,Glob,Grep"`，并留注释说明来龙去脉。

## Evidence

- 6/22、6/29 scheduled run success（v1.0.88）；#2737 于 7/6 06:22 UTC 合入，当天
10:33 UTC 的 scheduled run 起连挂。
- 对照组：`docs-maintenance.yml` 同为 v1.0.165 但带 `--allowedTools`，7/6、7/13 均
success。

## Validation

- Ruby Psych 解析改后 YAML 通过
- actionlint 仅报存量的 Blacksmith runner-label 忽略项（与 #2737 相同）
- `git diff --check` 通过
- 未在本地跑 workflow 本体（scheduled Bedrock 任务无法本地复现）；合入后可用
`workflow_dispatch` + `dry_run: true` 单模块彩排验证

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr_body:
## Summary

修复 weekly Architectural Code Review 连续两周（[2026-07-06](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/28785274130)、[2026-07-13](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/ecap-workspace/actions/runs/29241552938)）全 matrix 失败的问题。

**根因**：#2737 把 `claude-code-action` 从 v1.0.88 升到 v1.0.165 时，`claude-arch-review.yaml` 的 `claude_args` 只传了 `--model`，没有像 `docs-maintenance.yml` 一样补 `--allowedTools`。v1.0.165 的 agent 模式默认不再放行写工具，模型对 `findings.md` 的 Write 全部被 permission-denied（失败 run 的 SDK 结果里 `permission_denials_count: 9`），三个模块都挂在 "Normalize finding values" 的 `findings.md not produced by review step`。

**修复**：给 `claude_args` 补上与 `docs-maintenance.yml` 一致的 `--allowedTools "Bash,Edit,Write,Read,Glob,Grep"`，并留注释说明来龙去脉。

## Evidence

- 6/22、6/29 scheduled run success（v1.0.88）；#2737 于 7/6 06:22 UTC 合入，当天 10:33 UTC 的 scheduled run 起连挂。
- 对照组：`docs-maintenance.yml` 同为 v1.0.165 但带 `--allowedTools`，7/6、7/13 均 success。

## Validation

- Ruby Psych 解析改后 YAML 通过
- actionlint 仅报存量的 Blacksmith runner-label 忽略项（与 #2737 相同）
- `git diff --check` 通过
- 未在本地跑 workflow 本体（scheduled Bedrock 任务无法本地复现）；合入后可用 `workflow_dispatch` + `dry_run: true` 单模块彩排验证

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
pr_title: ci(arch-review): restore write tools via --allowedTools after action upgrade

message:
fix(agent-builder): 修复构建器布局、测试面板与发送按钮 (#2837)

## 改动说明

- 修复 Agent Builder 的单面板布局：未展开测试面板时，聊天会话和输入框保持线上宽度并居中显示，不再随页面横向拉伸
- 优化双面板布局：展开测试面板后，主构建区与测试区输入框保持相同高度，并移除多余分割线和测试区快捷按钮
- 完善测试面板交互：顶部按钮可展开或收起测试面板，并根据当前状态显示对应 tooltip
- 整理项目操作：移除顶部重复的 Share 入口，启用项目菜单内的 Share 功能
- 修复页面边缘的 More tooltip 被容器截断的问题
- 按 ZooWork Figma 设计还原发送按钮：32×32 容器、20×20 实心纸飞机图标，以及黑色激活态和灰色禁用态
- 完善本地 mock 的 Mattermost 身份与会话接口，使 Agent Builder 页面可以完整交互预览

## 问题原因

Agent Builder
复用了共享聊天组件的默认布局和图标，但单面板与双面板需要不同的宽度约束；测试面板两侧的输入框也缺少统一的高度契约。与此同时，顶部项目操作存在重复入口，tooltip
在靠近容器边缘时会被裁切，发送按钮仍使用通用线框箭头，未体现 ZooWork 设计中的实心纸飞机样式。

本地预览还依赖 Mattermost 用户身份和会话数据，原 mock 接口不完整时会让输入框和测试面板停留在连接状态，无法验证真实交互。

## 验证

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh <Agent Builder 相关改动文件>`
- [x] Agent Builder 相关 TypeScript、ESLint 和单元测试通过
- [x] 在 `/agent-builder/abp_mock_test` 验证单面板、双面板、输入框高度和测试面板交互
- [x] 浏览器计算样式确认左右发送按钮均为 32×32，使用 Figma 的 20×20 实心纸飞机图标
- [x] PR CI 41/41 通过

---------

Co-authored-by: Lynn Zhuang <lynnzhuang@MacBook-Pro-2.local>
pr_body:
## 改动说明

- 修复 Agent Builder 的单面板布局：未展开测试面板时，聊天会话和输入框保持线上宽度并居中显示，不再随页面横向拉伸
- 优化双面板布局：展开测试面板后，主构建区与测试区输入框保持相同高度，并移除多余分割线和测试区快捷按钮
- 完善测试面板交互：顶部按钮可展开或收起测试面板，并根据当前状态显示对应 tooltip
- 整理项目操作：移除顶部重复的 Share 入口，启用项目菜单内的 Share 功能
- 修复页面边缘的 More tooltip 被容器截断的问题
- 按 ZooWork Figma 设计还原发送按钮：32×32 容器、20×20 实心纸飞机图标，以及黑色激活态和灰色禁用态
- 完善本地 mock 的 Mattermost 身份与会话接口，使 Agent Builder 页面可以完整交互预览

## 问题原因

Agent Builder 复用了共享聊天组件的默认布局和图标，但单面板与双面板需要不同的宽度约束；测试面板两侧的输入框也缺少统一的高度契约。与此同时，顶部项目操作存在重复入口，tooltip 在靠近容器边缘时会被裁切，发送按钮仍使用通用线框箭头，未体现 ZooWork 设计中的实心纸飞机样式。

本地预览还依赖 Mattermost 用户身份和会话数据，原 mock 接口不完整时会让输入框和测试面板停留在连接状态，无法验证真实交互。

## 验证

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh <Agent Builder 相关改动文件>`

- [x] 浏览器计算样式确认左右发送按钮均为 32×32，使用 Figma 的 20×20 实心纸飞机图标
- [x] PR CI 41/41 通过

message:
fix(web): surface backend error detail in user-facing error messages (#2843)

## Summary
- Surface backend API error detail in user-facing error toasts/banners
instead of fixed generic text ("Failed to add agent. Please try again.",
"Network error. …"), across ~29 call sites: agents-manager
hire/fire/update/reset, publish/ZooSquare actions, pack purchase flow,
billing (subscription lifecycle, customer portal, invoices, plan renew),
settings data-permissions consent save, Antom payment confirmation poll,
chat interactive cards, onboarding companion select, restart prompt, and
the legacy task-failure bubble.
- New shared helper `withErrorDetail(fallback, err)` in
`web/app/src/lib/query/normalize-error.ts` composes `"<existing
localized/generic prefix>: <backend detail>"`, stripping the prefix's
trailing `.`/`。` to avoid an `".:"` seam; the generic text remains the
fallback when no detail is available (e.g. plain network failures).
`normalizeError` now also preserves plain-string errors so
result-envelope details (`data.error`) flow through the same helper.
- Deliberately unchanged: error boundaries (`ErrorBoundary`,
`app/error.tsx`, `global-error.tsx`) and SSE/WebSocket connection-loss
messaging, where generic wording is intentional and no usable detail
exists.

## Root cause
Both API layers already carry backend detail — `lib/api/backend.ts`
returns `error = data.error || data.detail || data.message || "HTTP
<status>"`, and `lib/api/errors.ts` throws `ApiError` with
message/code/status — but many call sites discarded it via bare `catch
{}`, discarded `(_err)` params, `onError: () => void` callback
signatures (publish hooks), or by reading `result.error` on one branch
but not the sibling branch (`useSubscriptionLifecycle`). Users only ever
saw the hardcoded generic string.

## Test plan
- [x] New unit tests for `withErrorDetail` (trailing-period strip, `。`
strip, no-detail passthrough) and string-preserving `normalizeError`
- [x] Updated regression tests across all touched surfaces assert the
composed `prefix: detail` messages and fallback behavior (241 tests in
the 12 touched spec files pass)
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh` green: guards + tsc + ESLint + full
unit suite (7,548 tests)
- [ ] CI (`code-quality / lint-and-test`)
pr_body:
## Summary
- Surface backend API error detail in user-facing error toasts/banners instead of fixed generic text ("Failed to add agent. Please try again.", "Network error. …"), across ~29 call sites: agents-manager hire/fire/update/reset, publish/ZooSquare actions, pack purchase flow, billing (subscription lifecycle, customer portal, invoices, plan renew), settings data-permissions consent save, Antom payment confirmation poll, chat interactive cards, onboarding companion select, restart prompt, and the legacy task-failure bubble.
- New shared helper `withErrorDetail(fallback, err)` in `web/app/src/lib/query/normalize-error.ts` composes `"<existing localized/generic prefix>: <backend detail>"`, stripping the prefix's trailing `.`/`。` to avoid an `".:"` seam; the generic text remains the fallback when no detail is available (e.g. plain network failures). `normalizeError` now also preserves plain-string errors so result-envelope details (`data.error`) flow through the same helper.
- Deliberately unchanged: error boundaries (`ErrorBoundary`, `app/error.tsx`, `global-error.tsx`) and SSE/WebSocket connection-loss messaging, where generic wording is intentional and no usable detail exists.

## Root cause
Both API layers already carry backend detail — `lib/api/backend.ts` returns `error = data.error || data.detail || data.message || "HTTP <status>"`, and `lib/api/errors.ts` throws `ApiError` with message/code/status — but many call sites discarded it via bare `catch {}`, discarded `(_err)` params, `onError: () => void` callback signatures (publish hooks), or by reading `result.error` on one branch but not the sibling branch (`useSubscriptionLifecycle`). Users only ever saw the hardcoded generic string.

## Test plan
- [x] New unit tests for `withErrorDetail` (trailing-period strip, `。` strip, no-detail passthrough) and string-preserving `normalizeError`
- [x] Updated regression tests across all touched surfaces assert the composed `prefix: detail` messages and fallback behavior (241 tests in the 12 touched spec files pass)
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh` green: guards + tsc + ESLint + full unit suite (7,548 tests)
- [ ] CI (`code-quality / lint-and-test`)
pr_title: fix(web): surface backend error detail in user-facing error messages

refactor(web): remove retired session history frontend (#2842)

## Summary

- remove the retired `/session-history` page, user-menu entry, settings
diary card, client hooks, API wrappers, and translations
- remove the unused `/api/session/archived/list` and
`/api/session/archived/history` Next.js BFF routes while preserving
active agent session navigation and backend routes
- add regression coverage that keeps the retired page and BFF routes
unavailable

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`

message:
feat(openclaw): add rate-limit diagnostics (#2840)

## Summary
- add structured diagnostics when OpenClaw per-user rate limits are
exceeded
- record the backend request method and safe FastAPI route template
- record sliding-window counts and retry timing without exposing them in
the HTTP response
- preserve the existing 30 requests per 5 seconds threshold

## Root cause
Existing 429 logs contained only the service error code and did not
identify the triggering backend route or sliding-window state. This made
ECA-1190 difficult to attribute from GCP logs after the event.

Raw URL paths are intentionally not logged because path parameters may
contain opaque access tokens. Previous requests in the same five-second
window can be correlated by UID using existing structured request logs.

## Validation
- 52 focused claw-interface unit tests
- full claw-interface Ruff format check
- Ruff
- Pyright
pr_body:
## Summary
- add structured diagnostics when OpenClaw per-user rate limits are exceeded
- record the backend request method and safe FastAPI route template
- record sliding-window counts and retry timing without exposing them in the HTTP response
- preserve the existing 30 requests per 5 seconds threshold

## Root cause
Existing 429 logs contained only the service error code and did not identify the triggering backend route or sliding-window state. This made ECA-1190 difficult to attribute from GCP logs after the event.

Raw URL paths are intentionally not logged because path parameters may contain opaque access tokens. Previous requests in the same five-second window can be correlated by UID using existing structured request logs.

## Validation
- 52 focused claw-interface unit tests
- full claw-interface Ruff format check
- Ruff
- Pyright

message:
feat(dashboard-console): add latest submission archive download to agent pack rows (#2841)

# Purpose

Operators reviewing the zooclaw agent pack catalogue had no way to
retrieve a pack's current archive from the dashboard console — auditing
what actually shipped meant digging keys out of R2 by hand. This adds a
per-row download action on the Agent Packs page that saves the latest
submission's archive through the existing SRP-gated `/api/r2/download`
route.

# Approach

- **View model owns the behavior** (`use-view-model.ts`): new
`handleDownloadArchive(pack)` resolves the latest submission's R2 key
via the existing `latestArchiveAssetId()` /
`archiveFilenameFromAssetId()` helpers (same resolution the
metadata-refresh flow uses), fetches through `downloadFileFromR2()`, and
saves via a temporary object-URL anchor click with revoke in `finally`.
A ref-based guard ignores clicks while a download is in flight;
`downloadingPackId` exposes per-row busy state.
- **Errors surface next to the table**: a dedicated `downloadError`
state (including the pack name) renders as a `role="alert"` notice above
the table — not the dialog-only `uploadError`, which would be invisible
during a row action.
- **Availability gated like the refresh action** (`route.tsx`): enabled
only when live backend data is present and the resolved asset id is
non-empty; in seed/offline mode the button is disabled since asset ids
are fake local keys.
- **Table stays presentational** (`pack-table.tsx`): a `DownloadIcon`
`IconButton` following the existing action-button conventions
(`aria-label` per pack, `title` tooltip, disabled styling).

# Tests

- View model: successful download calls `downloadFileFromR2` with the
latest submission's `asset_id` + derived filename, creates/revokes the
object URL, clicks the anchor, and clears busy state
- View model: failure sets a pack-named `downloadError` and clears busy
state; overlapping clicks while a download is pending are ignored; a
retry clears the previous error
- Table: download button rendered per row with accessible name; disabled
when ineligible and while any download is in progress

# Test plan

- [x] `pnpm run typecheck` (dashboard-console) — clean
- [x] `pnpm exec vitest run` (dashboard-console) — 66 files, 550 tests
passed
- [x] `pnpm exec eslint app/routes/agent-packs app/lib` — clean
- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test`
pr_body:
# Purpose

Operators reviewing the zooclaw agent pack catalogue had no way to retrieve a pack's current archive from the dashboard console — auditing what actually shipped meant digging keys out of R2 by hand. This adds a per-row download action on the Agent Packs page that saves the latest submission's archive through the existing SRP-gated `/api/r2/download` route.

# Approach

- **View model owns the behavior** (`use-view-model.ts`): new `handleDownloadArchive(pack)` resolves the latest submission's R2 key via the existing `latestArchiveAssetId()` / `archiveFilenameFromAssetId()` helpers (same resolution the metadata-refresh flow uses), fetches through `downloadFileFromR2()`, and saves via a temporary object-URL anchor click with revoke in `finally`. A ref-based guard ignores clicks while a download is in flight; `downloadingPackId` exposes per-row busy state.
- **Errors surface next to the table**: a dedicated `downloadError` state (including the pack name) renders as a `role="alert"` notice above the table — not the dialog-only `uploadError`, which would be invisible during a row action.
- **Availability gated like the refresh action** (`route.tsx`): enabled only when live backend data is present and the resolved asset id is non-empty; in seed/offline mode the button is disabled since asset ids are fake local keys.
- **Table stays presentational** (`pack-table.tsx`): a `DownloadIcon` `IconButton` following the existing action-button conventions (`aria-label` per pack, `title` tooltip, disabled styling).

# Tests

- View model: successful download calls `downloadFileFromR2` with the latest submission's `asset_id` + derived filename, creates/revokes the object URL, clicks the anchor, and clears busy state
- View model: failure sets a pack-named `downloadError` and clears busy state; overlapping clicks while a download is pending are ignored; a retry clears the previous error
- Table: download button rendered per row with accessible name; disabled when ineligible and while any download is in progress

# Test plan

- [x] `pnpm run typecheck` (dashboard-console) — clean
- [x] `pnpm exec vitest run` (dashboard-console) — 66 files, 550 tests passed
- [x] `pnpm exec eslint app/routes/agent-packs app/lib` — clean
- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test`
pr_title: feat(dashboard-console): add latest submission archive download to agent pack rows

message:
fix(chat-replay): preserve agent avatars in shared replays (#2826)

## Summary

- Preserve the real agent avatar when sharing replays from the main chat
and session thread.
- Fall back to the ownership-validated `AgentWorkspace.avatar_url` for
legacy clients, loading races, and Agent Builder shares.
- Keep client-provided identity avatars as the highest priority and
handle the 1024-character avatar URL limit consistently.

## Root cause

Replay snapshots only persisted `resolvedChatIdentity.avatar`. When that
value had not loaded yet, the live chat could still render
`activeAgentAvatarUrl`, but replay creation stored no avatar and the
public viewer fell back to the default brand avatar. The backend already
resolved the owning workspace but discarded its avatar URL.

Existing immutable replay snapshots are unchanged; newly created shares
use the corrected avatar resolution.

Linear:
https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-1214/agent-%E5%88%86%E4%BA%AB-replay-%E7%9A%84%E5%A4%B4%E5%83%8F%E6%94%B9%E6%88%90%E7%9C%9F%E5%AE%9E-agent-%E5%A4%B4%E5%83%8F

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Targeted frontend Vitest: 3 files, 88 tests passed
- [x] `pytest -q
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_chat_replay_create.py`: 36
passed
- [x] Commit and pre-push hooks: frontend lint/typecheck and backend
Ruff/Pyright/import contracts passed
pr_body:
## Summary

- Preserve the real agent avatar when sharing replays from the main chat and session thread.
- Fall back to the ownership-validated `AgentWorkspace.avatar_url` for legacy clients, loading races, and Agent Builder shares.
- Keep client-provided identity avatars as the highest priority and handle the 1024-character avatar URL limit consistently.

## Root cause

Replay snapshots only persisted `resolvedChatIdentity.avatar`. When that value had not loaded yet, the live chat could still render `activeAgentAvatarUrl`, but replay creation stored no avatar and the public viewer fell back to the default brand avatar. The backend already resolved the owning workspace but discarded its avatar URL.

Existing immutable replay snapshots are unchanged; newly created shares use the corrected avatar resolution.

Linear: https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-1214/agent-%E5%88%86%E4%BA%AB-replay-%E7%9A%84%E5%A4%B4%E5%83%8F%E6%94%B9%E6%88%90%E7%9C%9F%E5%AE%9E-agent-%E5%A4%B4%E5%83%8F

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- [x] Targeted frontend Vitest: 3 files, 88 tests passed
- [x] `pytest -q services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_chat_replay_create.py`: 36 passed
- [x] Commit and pre-push hooks: frontend lint/typecheck and backend Ruff/Pyright/import contracts passed
pr_title: fix(chat-replay): preserve agent avatars in shared replays
